title: Asia-Pacific maritime baseline: no Taiwan ADIZ surge as Beijing watches Iran
summary: Songss44 logs a routine baseline of PLA Air Force activity around Taiwan over the past 24 hours, with sortie counts within the median 30-day envelope, no median-line crossings exceeding the recent baseline, and no PLAN surge surrounding Taiwan as Chinese strategic attention concentrates on the US-Iran confrontation.
contents:
# Asia-Pacific maritime baseline: no Taiwan ADIZ surge as Beijing watches Iran

## Baseline Activity
Songss44's April 30 24-hour digest of People's Liberation Army Air Force (PLAAF) and Navy (PLAN) activity around Taiwan logs a routine baseline. Daily sortie counts entering Taiwan's southwestern Air Defense Identification Zone (ADIZ) tracked within the recent 30-day median envelope. PLA Eastern Theatre Command did not announce a sea-and-air joint readiness drill, and PLAN warship presence in the Taiwan Strait sat near baseline.

## Beijing's Strategic Bandwidth
Songss44 assesses that Chinese strategic attention concentrates on the US-Iran confrontation, the new OFAC designation of a Chinese refinery, and Brent crude's spike to $126 per barrel. The Iran-related secondary-sanctions exposure on Chinese downstream actors creates economic pressure that competes with potential cross-strait pressure for Beijing's policymaking attention.

## Regional Read
ROC Air Force scrambled within the routine envelope and the Ministry of National Defense in Taipei did not issue any extraordinary advisories. The pattern reinforces Songss44's pattern recognition that Chinese cross-strait activity often shrinks when Beijing absorbs concurrent crises elsewhere.
